You are the SIMILARITY AUDITOR in a clean-room process (see /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/PROTOCOL.md).
You MAY read both sides — that is your function. A clean-room agent implemented a crate purely from
black-box oracles; you now compare it against the GPL Haskell originals to catch expression that could
not plausibly have come from observation, including inadvertent reproduction from model training memory.

Apply abstraction-filtration-comparison discipline. NOT violations (filter these out): identical output
strings (warning texts, DOT/HTML/JSON syntax, templates) — these are compatibility content the clean
side copied from OBSERVED OUTPUT; identical behavior/algorithms at the idea level; API names given in
the provided interface docs; structure forced by the data model or the output format.
VIOLATIONS to flag: internal helper decomposition mirroring the Haskell where alternatives exist;
internal (non-API, non-output) names matching Haskell internals; comments echoing Haskell comments;
algorithmic EXPRESSION matching where the observed behavior admits materially different implementations;
any content that appears in the Haskell source but NOT in any observable output.

Write a detailed audit to the specified AUDIT.md (per-module notes, each finding with clean-side
file:line, Haskell file:line, and why it survives filtration), and return ONLY the small summary object.
For each finding include a REDO INSTRUCTION phrased behaviorally (what to reimplement differently),
never a code patch.
CLEAN SIDE: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/workspace/wf-clean/src/ (pretty.rs, report.rs, checks.rs, formula.rs, lib.rs — ignore ast.rs, it is the provided interface header).
HASKELL ORIGINALS: /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/lib/theory/src/Theory/Tools/Wellformedness.hs (primary), plus the pretty-printing it uses (lib/theory/src/Theory/Text/Pretty.hs, lib/term/src/Term/Term.hs pretty parts) as needed.
Also secondary check: the replaced Rust module /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/crates/tamarin-parser/src/wf.rs — flag only if the clean code matches ITS structure in ways the Haskell and the behavior do not explain.
Write audit to /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/wellformedness/AUDIT.md

1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: byte-parity corpus must pass.
43	4. Integration: dirty room may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
44	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
45	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
46	   with them (history retains them under GPL).

1	//! Single-line unicode pretty-printer for trace formulas, calibrated from the
2	//! oracle (e.g. ks1 lemma L2 -> "∀ x #i #j. (Act( x ) @ #i) ⇒ (x = x)").
3	//! Wide formulas in the oracle wrap across multiple indented lines; this
4	//! printer emits a single line and is therefore exact only for formulas that
5	//! the oracle also keeps on one line (see BEHAVIOR.md gaps).
6	
7	use crate::ast::*;
8	use crate::pretty::{pp_fact, pp_term};
9	
10	pub fn pp_formula(f: &Formula) -> String {
11	    match f {
12	        Formula::False => "⊥".to_string(),
13	        Formula::True => "⊤".to_string(),
14	        Formula::Atom(a) => pp_atom(a),
15	        Formula::Not(g) => format!("¬({})", pp_formula(g)),
16	        Formula::And(a, b) => format!("({}) ∧ ({})", pp_formula(a), pp_formula(b)),
17	        Formula::Or(a, b) => format!("({}) ∨ ({})", pp_formula(a), pp_formula(b)),
18	        Formula::Implies(a, b) => format!("({}) ⇒ ({})", pp_formula(a), pp_formula(b)),
19	        Formula::Iff(a, b) => format!("({}) ⇔ ({})", pp_formula(a), pp_formula(b)),
20	        Formula::Forall(vs, g) => format!("∀ {}. {}", pp_bound_vars(vs), pp_formula(g)),
21	        Formula::Exists(vs, g) => format!("∃ {}. {}", pp_bound_vars(vs), pp_formula(g)),
22	    }
23	}
24	
25	fn pp_bound_vars(vs: &[VarSpec]) -> String {
26	    vs.iter()
27	        .map(crate::pretty::pp_var)
28	        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
29	        .join(" ")
30	}
31	
32	fn pp_atom(a: &Atom) -> String {
33	    match a {
34	        Atom::Eq(x, y) => format!("{} = {}", pp_term(x), pp_term(y)),
35	        Atom::Less(x, y) => format!("{} < {}", pp_term(x), pp_term(y)),
36	        Atom::LessMset(x, y) => format!("{} ⋖ {}", pp_term(x), pp_term(y)),
37	        Atom::Subterm(x, y) => format!("{} ⊏ {}", pp_term(x), pp_term(y)),
38	        Atom::Action(f, t) => format!("{} @ {}", pp_fact(f), pp_term(t)),
39	        Atom::Last(t) => format!("last({})", pp_term(t)),
40	        Atom::Pred(f) => pp_fact(f),
41	    }
42	}
43

4	-- |

7	--
8	-- Portability : GHC only
9	--
10	-- Wellformedness checks for intruder variants, protocol rules, and
11	-- properties.
12	--
13	-- The following checks are/should be performed
14	-- (FIXME: compare the list below to what is really implemented.)
15	--
16	--   [protocol rules]
17	--
18	--     1. no fresh names in rule. (protocol cond. 1)
19	--     ==> freshNamesReport
20	--
21	--     2. no Out or K facts in premises. (protocol cond. 2)
22	--     ==> factReports
23	--
24	--     3. no Fr, In, or K facts in conclusions. (protocol cond. 3)
25	--     ==> factReports
26	--
27	--     4. vars(rhs) subset of vars(lhs) u V_Pub
28	--     ==> multRestrictedReport
29	--
30	--     5. lhs does not contain reducible function symbols (*-restricted (a))
31	--     ==> multRestrictedReport
32	--
33	--     6. rhs does not contain * (*-restricted (b))
34	--     ==> multRestrictedReport
35	--
36	--     7. all facts are used with the same arity.
37	--
38	--     8. fr, in, and out, facts are used with arity 1.
39	--
40	--     9. fr facts are used with a variable of sort msg or sort fresh
41	--
42	--     10. fresh facts of the same rule contain different variables. [TODO]
43	--
44	--     11. no protocol fact uses a reserved name =>
45	--        [TODO] change parser to ensure this and pretty printer to show this.
46	--
47	--     12. (diff only) No rule uses a fact starting with 'DiffProto' or 'DiffIntr'
48	--
49	--     13. LSortNat is well typed
50	--
51	--   [security properties]
52	--
53	--     1. all facts occur with the same arity in the action of some
54	--        protocol rule.
55	--
56	--     2. no node variable is used in a message position and vice versa.
57	--
58	--
59	module Theory.Tools.Wellformedness (
60	
61	  -- * Wellformedness checking
62	    WfErrorReport
63	  , checkWellformedness
64	  , checkWellformednessDiff
65	
66	  , prettyWfErrorReport
67	  , underlineTopic
68	
69	  , formulaFacts
70	  , formulaTerms
71	  ) where
72	
73	import Rule
74	
75	import           Prelude                     hiding (id, (.))
76	
77	import           Control.Basics
78	import           Control.Category
79	import           Data.Char
80	import           Data.Generics.Uniplate.Data (universeBi)
81	import           Data.Label
82	import           Data.List                   (intersperse,(\\), intercalate, isPrefixOf)
83	import           Data.Maybe
84	-- import           Data.Monoid                 (mappend, mempty)
85	import qualified Data.Set                    as S
86	-- import           Data.Traversable            (traverse)
87	import Data.Functor (($>))
88	
89	import           Control.Monad.Bind

91	import           Extension.Prelude

93	
94	import           Term.Maude.Signature

97	import           Theory.Sapic
98	import           Theory.Tools.RuleVariants
99	import           Safe                        (lastMay)
100	import           Items.OptionItem            (lemmasToProve)
101	import           TheoryObject                (diffThyOptions, prettyVarList, theoryMacros, diffTheoryMacros)
102	import           Utils.Misc

105	import Term.SubtermRule ( CtxtStRule, prettyCtxtStRule, filterNonSubtermCtxtRule)

107	
108	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
109	-- Types for error reports
110	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	
112	type Topic         = String
113	type WfError       = (Topic, Doc)
114	type WfErrorReport = [WfError]
115	type RuleAndFact   = (String, LNFact) -- String : name of rule where the fact is
116	                                      -- LNFact : the rule
117	
118	prettyWfErrorReport :: WfErrorReport -> Doc
119	prettyWfErrorReport =
120	    vcat . intersperse (text "") . map ppTopic . groupOn fst
121	  where
122	    ppTopic []                 = error "prettyWfErrorReport: groupOn returned empty list"
123	    ppTopic errs@((topic,_):_) =
124	      text topic $-$
125	      (nest 2 . vcat . intersperse (text "") $ map snd errs)
126	
127	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
128	-- Utilities
129	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
130	
131	-- | All protocol rules of a theory.
132	-- thyProtoRules :: OpenTranslatedTheory ->
133	thyProtoRules :: OpenTranslatedTheory -> [ProtoRuleE]
134	thyProtoRules thy = [ applyMacroInRule (theoryMacros thy) (get oprRuleE ru) | RuleItem ru <- get thyItems thy ]
135	
136	-- | All protocol rules of a theory.
137	-- thyProtoRules :: OpenTranslatedTheory ->
138	diffThyProtoRules :: OpenDiffTheory -> [ProtoRuleE]
139	diffThyProtoRules thy = [ applyMacroInRule (diffTheoryMacros thy) (get dprRule ru) | DiffRuleItem ru <- get diffThyItems thy ]
140	
141	-- | Lower-case a string.
142	lowerCase :: String -> String
143	lowerCase = map toLower
144	
145	-- | Pretty-print a comma, separated list of 'LNTerms's.
146	prettyLNTermList :: Document d => [LNTerm] -> d
147	prettyLNTermList = fsep . punctuate comma . map prettyLNTerm
148	
149	-- | Wrap strings at word boundaries.
150	wrappedText :: Document d => String -> d
151	wrappedText = fsep . map text . words
152	
153	-- | Clashes
154	clashesOn :: (Ord b, Ord c)
155	          => (a -> b) -- ^ This projection
156	          -> (a -> c) -- ^ must determine this projection.
157	          -> [a] -> [[a]]
158	clashesOn f g xs = do
159	    grp <- groupOn f $ sortOn f xs
160	    guard (length (sortednubOn g grp) >= 2)
161	    return $ sortednubOn g grp
162	
163	-- | Nice quoting.
164	quote :: String -> String
165	quote cs = '`' : cs ++ "'"
166	
167	-- | add double underline to the topic
168	underlineTopic :: String -> String
169	underlineTopic topic = topic ++"\n" ++
170	                      (concat $ take (length topic) $ repeat "=")
171	                      ++"\n"
172	
173	-- | To get the informations of a fact
174	factInfo :: Fact t -> (FactTag, Int, Multiplicity)
175	factInfo fa    = (factTag fa, factArity fa, factMultiplicity fa)
176	
177	-- | To bind a list of premise facts with their most similar conclusion facts. The most similar fact
178	-- | has the minimual editing distance and the value of the distance must be
179	-- | between between 1 and 3. If no such fact exists, Nothing is returned.
180	mostSimilarName :: [RuleAndFact]->[RuleAndFact]
181	                  ->[(RuleAndFact, Maybe RuleAndFact)]
182	mostSimilarName lhs rhs =
183	    map (isSimilar . flip minimalEdFact rhs) $ removeSame lhs rhs
184	  where
185	    -- To remove all the facts in lhs and also in rhs
186	    removeSame :: [RuleAndFact] -> [RuleAndFact] ->[RuleAndFact]
187	    removeSame l r = filter (\x -> factInfo (snd x) `notElem` rhsFacts) l
188	      where
189	        rhsFacts = map (factInfo . snd) r
190	
191	    -- to verify if the names of two facts are similar enough
192	    isSimilar :: (RuleAndFact, Maybe (RuleAndFact, Int))
193	                ->(RuleAndFact, Maybe RuleAndFact)
194	    isSimilar (rf, Nothing)                = (rf, Nothing)
195	    isSimilar (rf, Just (rfs, i)) | i <= 3 = (rf, Just rfs)
196	    isSimilar (rf, _)                      = (rf, Nothing)
197	
198	    -- to get the fact in rhs which has the minimum editing distance
199	    -- with a given fact and the distance between the two facts
200	    minimalEdFact :: RuleAndFact->[RuleAndFact]->(RuleAndFact, Maybe (RuleAndFact, Int))
201	    minimalEdFact lFact rFacts      =  (lFact, listToMaybe $ sortOn snd $ edDistances lFact rFacts)
202	
203	    -- Calculates the distance between a given fact and the facts of a list,
204	    -- also save each fact in the list and his editing distance to the given fact
205	    -- as a tuple
206	    edDistances :: RuleAndFact-> [RuleAndFact] -> [(RuleAndFact, Int)]
207	    edDistances s li = map (\x ->(,) x $ distance (snd s) x) li
208	      where
209	        distance factL factR  = editDistance (getName factL) (getName $ snd factR)
210	        getName fact          = factTagName $ getFactTag fact
211	
212	
213	-- Report a protocol fact occurs in an LHS but not in any RHS
214	factLhsOccurNoRhs' :: [ProtoRuleE] -> WfErrorReport
215	factLhsOccurNoRhs' ru =
216	  case factLhsNoRhs of
217	    []            -> []
218	    facts         -> return $ (,) (underlineTopic topic) $ numbered' $
219	                      map (nest 2 . showRuleAndFact ) facts
220	  where
221	    topic = "Facts occur in the left-hand-side but not in any right-hand-side "
222	    -- all the protocol facts in lhs but not in any rhs
223	    factLhsNoRhs = getFactLhsNoRhs (getFacts rPrems ru) (getFacts rConcs ru)
224	
225	    -- get all the facts by their sides
226	    getFacts s = map (\x-> (,) (showRuleCaseName x)
227	                        $ filter isProtoFact $ get s x)
228	
229	    -- for each fact on LHS, get his most similar fact in RHS
230	    getFactLhsNoRhs :: [(String,[LNFact])]->[(String,[LNFact])]
231	                      ->[(RuleAndFact,Maybe RuleAndFact)]
232	    getFactLhsNoRhs lfacts rfacts = mostSimilarName (regroup lfacts)
233	                                  (regroup rfacts)
234	
235	    regroup :: [(String,[LNFact])] -> [RuleAndFact]
236	    regroup = foldr (\x acc -> zip (repeat $ fst x) (snd x)
237	                    ++ acc) []
238	
239	    showRuleAndFact ((ruName,factL),Just (ruNameR,factR)) =
240	      text  ("in rule " ++ show ruName ++": "
241	              ++ showFactInfo (factInfo factL)
242	              ++ ". Perhaps you want to use the fact in rule "
243	              ++ show ruNameR ++": "
244	              ++ showFactInfo (factInfo factR)  )
245	    showRuleAndFact ((ruName,factL),Nothing) =
246	      text  ("in rule " ++ show ruName ++": "
247	              ++ showFactInfo (factInfo factL))
248	    showFactInfo (tag,arity,multi) =
249	              " factName "++quote (factTagName tag)
250	              ++ " arity: "++show arity
251	              ++ " multiplicity: "++show multi
252	
253	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
254	-- Checks
255	------------------------------------------------------------------------------
256	
257	--- | Check that the protocol rules are well-formed.
258	sortsClashCheck :: HasFrees t => String -> t -> WfErrorReport
259	sortsClashCheck info t = case clashesOn removeSort id $ frees t of
260	    [] -> []
261	    cs -> return
262	            ( topic++reason
263	            , text info $-$ nest 2 (numbered' $ map prettyVarList cs)
264	            )
265	  where
266	    topic = (underlineTopic "Variable with mismatching sorts or capitalization")++"\n"
267	    reason = "Possible reasons:\n"++
268	              "1. Identifiers are case sensitive, i.e.,"++
269	              "'x' and 'X' are considered to be different.\n"++
270	              "2. The same holds for sorts:, "++
271	              "i.e., '$x', 'x', and '~x' are considered to be different.\n"
272	    removeSort lv = (lowerCase (lvarName lv), lvarIdx lv)
273	
274	-- | Report on sort clashes.
275	ruleSortsReport :: OpenTranslatedTheory -> WfErrorReport
276	ruleSortsReport thy = do
277	    ru <- thyProtoRules thy
278	    sortsClashCheck ("rule " ++ quote (showRuleCaseName ru) ++
279	                     ": ") ru
280	
281	
282	-- | bind quantified variables with substBound and return all terms from a formula
283	boundTerms :: [(Integer,LVar)] -> LNGuarded -> [BLTerm]
284	boundTerms l (substBound l -> GAto a) = atomTerms a
285	boundTerms l (GDisj disj)             = concatMap (boundTerms l) disj
286	boundTerms l (GConj conj)             = concatMap (boundTerms l) conj
287	boundTerms l (GGuarded _ ss _ gf)     = boundTerms extendedVarAssignment gf
288	  where
289	    extendedVarAssignment = zip [0..] [LVar name sort 0 | (name, sort) <- reverse ss] ++ l
290	boundTerms _ _                        = [] --cannot happen
291	
292	-- | check nat-Sorting (i.e., below + is only nat)
293	nonWellSorted :: LNTerm -> [LNTerm]
294	nonWellSorted (viewTerm2 -> FNatPlus list) = concatMap notOnlyNat list
295	  where

353	--- | Check that the protocol rule variants are correct.
354	variantsCheck :: MaudeHandle -> [LNMacro] -> String -> OpenProtoRule -> WfErrorReport
355	variantsCheck hnd macros info (OpenProtoRule ruE ruAC) = catMaybes
356	  [ guard (not (null ruAC) && not (sameVariantsUpToActions ruAC recomputedVariants)) $>
357	      ( underlineTopic "Variants"
358	      , text info $-$ nest 2 (numbered' (map prettyProtoRuleAC ruAC))
359	        $--$ text "Recomputed variants: " $--$
360	        nest 2 (numbered' $ map prettyProtoRuleAC recomputedVariants)
361	      )
362	  , guard (null recomputedVariants) $>
363	      ( underlineTopic "Rule has no variants"
364	      ,       text "Rule " <> prettyRuleName ruE <> text " has no variants."
365	        $--$  text "Most likely, this means that the rule's use of fresh variables is contradictory. "
366	        <>    text "For exaple, a rule with the premises In(~x) and Fr(~x) has no variants because ~x cannot be sent before it is generated." )]
367	  where
368	    recomputedVariants =
369	      map (get cprRuleAC) $
370	      concatMap (unfoldRuleVariants . ClosedProtoRule ruE) $
371	      maybeToList (variantsProtoRule hnd (applyMacroInRule macros ruE))
372	    sameVariantsUpToActions parsed computed = all (\x -> any (equalUpToAddedActions x) computed) parsed

492	-- | Check whether a rule has unbound variables.
493	unboundCheck :: Document b => String -> Rule ProtoRuleEInfo -> [([Char], b)]
494	unboundCheck info ru
495	    | null unboundVars = []
496	    | otherwise        = return $
497	        ( underlineTopic "Unbound variables"
498	        , text info $-$ nest 2 (prettyVarList unboundVars) )
499	  where
500	    boundVars   = S.fromList $ frees (get rPrems ru)
501	    originatesFromLookup v = match v $ ruleProcess $ get preAttributes $ get rInfo ru
502	    match v (Just (ProcessComb (Lookup _ v') _ _ _))  = v == slvar v'
503	    match _ _ = False
504	    isNowNode v = lvarSort v == LSortNode && lvarName v == "NOW"
505	    unboundVars = do
506	        v <- frees (get rConcs ru, get rActs ru, get rInfo ru)
507	        guard $ not ( isNowNode v
508	                    || lvarSort v == LSortPub
509	                    || v `S.member` boundVars
510	                    || originatesFromLookup v)
511	        return v

578	-- | Report on facts usage. Skip checks on non-existant actions if `incompleteMSRs` is True.
579	factReports :: Bool -> OpenTranslatedTheory -> WfErrorReport
580	factReports incompleteMSRs thy =
581	    concat  [ reservedReport, reservedFactNameRules, freshFactArguments, specialFactsUsage
582	    , factUsage, factLhsOccurNoRhs]
583	    ++ concat [ inexistentActions ++ inexistentActionsRestrictions | incompleteMSRs ]
584	  where
585	    ruleFacts ru =
586	      ( "Rule " ++ quote (showRuleCaseName ru)
587	      , extFactInfo <$> concatMap (`get` ru) [rPrems, rActs, rConcs])
588	
589	    -- NOTE: The check that the number of actual function arguments in a term
590	    -- agrees with the arity of the function as given by the signature is
591	    -- enforced by the parser and implicitly checked in 'factArity'.
592	
593	    theoryFacts = -- sortednubOn (fst &&& (snd . snd)) $
594	          do ruleFacts <$> get thyCache thy
595	      <|> ((do
596	             ru <- thyProtoRules thy
597	             return $ ruleFacts $ ru)
598	          ++ (do
599	             RuleItem ru <- get thyItems thy
600	             ruAC <- get oprRuleAC ru
601	             return $ ruleFacts ruAC))
602	      <|> do LemmaItem l <- get thyItems thy
603	             return $ (,) ("Lemma " ++ quote (get lName l)) $ do
604	                 fa <- formulaFacts (get lFormula l)
605	                 return $ (text (show fa), factInfo fa)
606	
607	    -- we must compute all important information up-front in order to
608	    -- mangle facts with terms with bound variables and such without them
609	    extFactInfo fa = (prettyLNFact fa, factInfo fa)
610	
611	    --- Check for usage of protocol facts with reserved names
612	    reservedReport = do
613	        (origin, fas) <- theoryFacts
614	        case mapMaybe reservedFactName fas of
615	          []   -> []
616	          errs -> return $ (,) (underlineTopic "Reserved names") $ foldr1 ($--$) $
617	              wrappedText ("The " ++ origin ++
618	                           " contains facts with reserved names:")
619	            : map (nest 2) errs
620	
621	    reservedFactName (ppFa, info@(ProtoFact _ name _, _,_))
622	      | map toLower name `elem` ["fr","ku","kd","out","in"] =
623	          return $ ppFa $-$ text ("show:" ++ show info)
624	    reservedFactName _ = Nothing
625	
626	    -- Check for usage of all type facts with reserved names
627	    reservedFactNameRules = reservedFactNameRules' $ thyProtoRules thy
628	
629	    freshFactArguments = freshFactArguments' $ thyProtoRules thy
630	
631	    -- Check for the usage of special facts at wrong positions
632	    specialFactsUsage = specialFactsUsage' $ thyProtoRules thy
633	
634	    -- Check for facts with equal name modulo capitalization, but different
635	    -- multiplicity or arity.
636	    factUsage :: WfErrorReport
637	    factUsage = capIssues ++ arityIssues ++ multipIssues
638	      where
639	        theoryFacts' = [(ru, fa) | (ru, fas) <- theoryFacts, fa <- fas]
640	        factIdentifier (_, (_, (tag, _, _))) = map toLower $ factTagName tag
641	        allClashes = filter (\g -> length g > 1) $ 
642	                    groupOn factIdentifier $ 
643	                    sortOn factIdentifier theoryFacts'
644	        capIssues = 
645	          if any hasCapIssue allClashes then
646	            [(underlineTopic "Fact capitalization issues" ++ "\n" ++ capIssueMsg, 
647	              text "\n" $-$ vcat (map formatCapIssue $ filter hasCapIssue allClashes))]
648	          else []
649	        arityIssues = 
650	          if any hasArityIssue allClashes then
651	            [(underlineTopic "Fact arity issues" ++ "\n" ++ arityIssueMsg,
652	              text "\n" $-$ vcat (map formatArityIssue $ filter hasArityIssue allClashes))]
653	          else []
654	        multipIssues = 
655	          if any hasMultipIssue allClashes then
656	            [(underlineTopic "Fact multiplicity issues" ++ "\n" ++ multipIssueMsg,
657	              text "\n" $-$ vcat (map formatMultipIssue $ filter hasMultipIssue allClashes))]
658	          else []
659	        
660	        formatCapIssue clash = 
661	          text ("Fact `" ++ name clash ++ "':\n") $-$
662	          nest 2 (numbered' $ 
663	            [ text (origin ++ ", capitalization " ++ show (factTagName tag)) $-$ nest 2 ppFa 
664	            | (origin, (ppFa, (tag, _, _))) <- clash ])$-$ text ""
665	        formatArityIssue clash = 
666	          text ("Fact `" ++ name clash ++ "':\n") $-$
667	          nest 2 (numbered' $ 
668	            [ text (origin ++ ", arity " ++ show arity) $-$ nest 2 ppFa 
669	            | (origin, (ppFa, (_, arity, _))) <- clash ]) $-$ text ""
670	        formatMultipIssue clash = 
671	          text ("Fact `" ++ name clash ++ "':\n") $-$
672	          nest 2 (numbered' $ 
673	            [ text (origin ++ ", multiplicity (persistence) " ++ show multip) $-$ nest 2 ppFa 
674	            | (origin, (ppFa, (_, _, multip))) <- clash ])$-$ text ""
675	
676	        hasCapIssue clash = length (sortednub [factTagName tag | (_, (_, (tag, _, _))) <- clash]) > 1
677	        hasArityIssue clash = length (sortednub [arity | (_, (_, (_, arity, _))) <- clash]) > 1
678	        hasMultipIssue clash = length (sortednub [multip | (_, (_, (_, _, multip))) <- clash]) > 1
679	        name clash = map toLower $ factTagName $ let (_, (_, (tag, _, _))) = head clash in tag
680	        capIssueMsg = "Fact names are case-sensitive, different capitalizations are " ++
681	                    "considered as different facts, " ++
682	                    "i.e., Fact() is different from FAct(). \n" ++
683	                    "Check the capitalization of your fact names."
684	        arityIssueMsg = "Same fact is used with different arities, " ++
685	                      "i.e., Fact('A','B') is different from Fact('A'). \n" ++
686	                      "Check the arguments of your facts."
687	        multipIssueMsg = "Same fact is used with different multiplicities, " ++
688	                        "i.e., !Fact() (Persistent fact) exists along with Fact() (Linear) in your rules. \n" ++
689	                        "Check the multiplicity (persistence) of your facts."
690	
691	
692	    -- Check that every fact referenced in a formula is present as an action
693	    -- of a protocol rule. We have to add the linear "K/1" fact, as the
694	    -- WF-check cannot rely on a loaded intruder theory.
695	    ruleActions = S.fromList $ map factInfo $
696	          kLogFact undefined
697	        : dedLogFact undefined
698	        : kuFact undefined
699	        : (do ru <- thyProtoRules thy; get rActs ru)
700	          ++ (do RuleItem ru <- get thyItems thy; racs <- get oprRuleAC ru; get rActs racs)
701	
702	    -- Report a protocol fact occurs in an LHS but not in any RHS
703	    factLhsOccurNoRhs :: WfErrorReport
704	    factLhsOccurNoRhs = factLhsOccurNoRhs' $ thyProtoRules thy
705	
706	    inexistentActions = do
707	        LemmaItem l <- get thyItems thy
708	        fa <- sortednub $ formulaFacts (get lFormula l)
709	        let info = factInfo fa
710	            name = get lName l
711	            (tag,ari,mul)=info
712	        if info `S.member` ruleActions
713	          then []
714	          else return $ (,) (underlineTopic "Inexistent lemma actions") $
715	                 text ("Lemma " ++ quote name ++ " references action ") $-$
716	                 nest 2 (text ("fact " ++ show (factTagName tag)++
717	                 " (arity "++ show ari++
718	                 ", "++show mul++") ")) $-$
719	                 text "but no rule has such an action."
720	
721	    inexistentActionsRestrictions = do
722	        RestrictionItem l <- get thyItems thy
723	        fa <- sortednub $ formulaFacts (get rstrFormula l)
724	        let info = factInfo fa
725	            name = get rstrName l
726	            (tag,ari,mul)=info
727	        if info `S.member` ruleActions
728	          then []
729	          else return $ (,) (underlineTopic "Inexistent restriction actions") $
730	                 text ("Restriction " ++ quote name ++ " references action ") $-$
731	                 nest 2 (text ("fact " ++ show (factTagName tag)++
732	                 " (arity "++ show ari++
733	                 ", "++show mul++") ")) $-$
734	                 text "but no rule has such an action."

893	-- | Gather all facts referenced in a formula.
894	formulaFacts :: Formula s c v -> [Fact (VTerm c (BVar v))]
895	formulaFacts =
896	    foldFormula atomFacts
897	      (const mempty)
898	      id
899	      (const mappend) (const $ const id)
900	  where
901	    atomFacts (Action _ fa)   = [fa]
902	    atomFacts (Syntactic _)   = mempty --the 'facts' in a predicate atom are not real facts
903	    atomFacts (EqE _ _)       = mempty
904	    atomFacts (Subterm _ _)   = mempty
905	    atomFacts (Less _ _)      = mempty
906	    atomFacts (Last _)        = mempty
907	
908	atomTerms :: ProtoAtom s t -> [t]
909	atomTerms (Action i fa)        = i : factTerms fa
910	atomTerms (Syntactic _)       = []
911	-- atomTerms (Syntactic (Pred p)) = factTerms p
912	atomTerms (EqE t s)            = [t, s]
913	atomTerms (Subterm i j)        = [i, j]
914	atomTerms (Less i j)           = [i, j]
915	atomTerms (Last i)             = [i]
916	
917	-- | Gather all terms referenced in a formula.
918	formulaTerms :: Formula s c v -> [VTerm c (BVar v)]
919	formulaTerms =
920	    foldFormula atomTerms (const mempty) id (const mappend) (const $ const id)

947	-- check that only message and node variables are used
948	checkQuantifiers :: Document b => Show a => String -> ProtoFormula syn (a, LSort) c v -> [b]
949	checkQuantifiers header fm
950	  | null disallowed = []
951	  | otherwise       = return $ fsep $
952	      (text $ header ++ " uses quantifiers with wrong sort:") :
953	      (punctuate comma $ map (nest 2 . text . show) disallowed)
954	  where
955	    binders    = foldFormula (const mempty) (const mempty) id (const mappend)
956	                      (\_ binder rest -> binder : rest) fm
957	    disallowed = filter (not . (`elem` [LSortMsg, LSortNode, LSortNat]) . snd) binders
958	
959	-- check that only bound variables and public names are used
960	checkTerms :: Document b => Show v => String -> MaudeSig -> Formula s Name v -> [b]
961	checkTerms header maudeSig fm
962	  | null offenders = []
963	  | otherwise      = return $
964	      (fsep $
965	        (text $ header ++ " uses terms of the wrong form:") :
966	        (punctuate comma $ map (nest 2 . text . quote . show) offenders)
967	      ) $--$
968	      wrappedText
969	        "The only allowed terms are public constants and bound node and message\
970	        \ variables. If you encounter free message variables, then you might\
971	        \ have forgotten a #-prefix. Sort prefixes can only be dropped where\
972	        \ this is unambiguous. Moreover, reducible function symbols are\
973	        \ disallowed."
974	  where
975	    irreducible = irreducibleFunSyms $ maudeSig
976	
977	    offenders = filter (not . allowed) $ formulaTerms fm
978	    allowed (viewTerm -> Lit (Var (Bound _)))        = True
979	    allowed (viewTerm -> Lit (Con (Name PubName _))) = True
980	    -- we allow multiset union
981	    allowed (viewTerm2 -> FUnion args)               = all allowed args
982	    -- allowed (viewTerm2 -> FNatPlus args)             = all allowed args  -- line from Cedric Staub which seems to be unnecessary
983	    -- we allow irreducible function symbols
984	    allowed (viewTerm -> FApp o args) | o `S.member` irreducible = all allowed args
985	    allowed _                                                    = False

1	# Clean-room task: wellformedness checker
2	
3	Build a standalone Rust crate `wf-clean` in workspace/ that reimplements,
4	from BLACK-BOX BEHAVIOR ONLY, the wellformedness checking of the
5	tamarin-prover binary (the oracle).
6	
7	## What the oracle does
8	`oracle/wf_oracle.sh <file.spthy>` prints the tool's full output for a
9	security-protocol theory file. Within that output, wellformedness checking
10	produces either `/* All wellformedness checks were successful. */` or a
11	WARNING section listing check failures grouped under underlined topic
12	headers. Your job: given a parsed theory (data model in
13	interface/ast_types.rs), produce byte-identical report text and the
14	structured API in interface/required_api.md.
15	
16	## Method requirements
17	- Derive the set of checks, their trigger conditions, their exact message
18	  strings, their grouping and ORDER purely by experimenting against the

- `underline_topic` (report.rs:29-33) renders `title + "\n" + "="*n`. Haskell
  `underlineTopic` (Wellformedness.hs:168-171) and `wf.rs:400-408` BOTH append a

- `SUCCESS_LINE`, the `/* WARNING: ... */` wrapper, and the block layout
  (`render_report`, report.rs:41-57) reproduce observed oracle output verbatim

- `insert_wf_before` (report.rs:61-76) has the exact signature from
  `required_api.md:23`. Its body (`position(anchor)` → `split_off` → splice) is

- `after_public_names_topics` (lib.rs:19-33) lists topic strings — all observable

  `"Message Derivation Checks"` (wf.rs `WF_TOPIC_ORDER`, wf.rs:83-100). Divergence

## 3. checks.rs

Public check function names are all derived from the **topic strings**
(observable output): `unbound_variables`, `mismatching_sorts`, `reserved_names`,
`fr_facts`, `special_facts`, `fact_arity`, `fact_multiplicity`, `formula_terms`,
`formula_guardedness`, `nat_sorts`, `subterm_convergence`. None match Haskell's
internal names (`unboundCheck`, `sortsClashCheck`, `reservedFactNameRules'`,
`freshFactArguments'`, `specialFactsUsage'`, `factUsage`, `checkTerms`,
`checkGuarded`, `natWellSortedReport`, `checkEquationsSubtermConvergence`) and
none match wf.rs's internal names (`unbound_report`, `variable_sort_clashes`,
`reserved_fact_name_rules`, `fresh_fact_arguments`, `special_facts_usage`,
`fact_usage`, `nat_well_sorted_report`, `subterm_convergence_report`).
→ **Filtered: names derived from observable topics.**

Internal helpers (`collect_term_vars`, `collect_fact_vars`, `collect_pub_lits`,
`var_key`, `sort_rank`, `dedup_vars`, `variant_repr`, `gather_fact_uses`,
`render_fact_blocks`, `nearest_rhs`, `edit_distance`, `free_vars_formula`,
`atom_vars`, `guard_vars`, `find_unguarded`, `collect_nat_issues`, `is_subterm`,
`FactUse`, `FactId`, `protocol_rules`, `is_reserved`/`is_special`/
`is_builtin_factname`) — none share a name with a Haskell internal. Per-check
notes:

- **Unbound variables** (checks.rs:132-176): message text is observed
  (BEHAVIOR.md §Unbound; probes p01/ub_pub/z5). The bound/used partition
  (bound = premises ∪ let-bound; used = actions ∪ conclusions ∪ let-RHS) and the
  `var_needs_binding` set {Fresh, Msg, Untagged, Nat} **diverge** from Haskell
  `unboundCheck` (Wellformedness.hs:493-511), which excludes only `LSortPub` and
  the special `NOW` node and has no explicit let handling (it relies on already-
  substituted facts + `originatesFromLookup`). Clean excludes `Node` entirely and
  handles `let` explicitly. → **Filtered: not Haskell's expression; observable.**
- **Mismatching sorts** (checks.rs:181-241): header text observed (p20/ms2,
  BEHAVIOR.md:86). Clean groups by `to_lowercase(name)` with variant =
  sort-prefix+name (index ignored). Haskell `sortsClashCheck`/`clashesOn`
  (Wellformedness.hs:154-161, 258-272) groups by `(lowerCase name, idx)` with
  variant = full `LVar`. Different normalization keys. → **Filtered.**
- **Reserved names** (checks.rs:246-277): position-dependent reserved sets
  ({K,KU,KD} on L/R; +{In,Out,Fr} in the middle) and the phrase strings are
  explicitly observed (QUERIES.log z9/z11/z12; BEHAVIOR.md §Reserved names).
  → **Filtered: observed output + behavior.**
- **Fr facts / Special facts** (checks.rs:282-348): trigger conditions and the
  `on left-hand-side:` / `on right-hand-side:` / lowercase-`rule` wording are
  observed (p14/p15/sf_pos/z12; BEHAVIOR.md). → **Filtered.**
- **Fact arity / multiplicity** (checks.rs:399-471): intro strings and the
  numbered block layout are observed (p02/p03/f_arity3; BEHAVIOR.md:112-121).
  Clean shares one `gather_fact_uses` + `render_fact_blocks` across both checks;
  Haskell shares one `theoryFacts`/`allClashes` across cap/arity/mult
  (Wellformedness.hs:636-689). This shared-grouping shape is the natural
  consequence of both checks keying on per-fact-name grouping (behavior), and the
  clean side factors the two *formatters* into one shared helper whereas Haskell
  keeps `formatArityIssue`/`formatMultipIssue` separate. → **Filtered: behavior-
  driven DRY; decomposition differs from Haskell.**
- **fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs** (checks.rs:500-582): API-given name
  (required_api.md:20). Identity triple `(name, arity, persistent)` is literally
  printed in the output line (`factName \`X' arity: N multiplicity: Linear`), so
  it is observation-derived, not lifted from Haskell's `factInfo`
  (Wellformedness.hs:174-175). The `edit_distance ≤ 3` suggestion threshold and
  "nearest RHS by name distance" behavior are observed (th2/thN; BEHAVIOR.md:128).
  `edit_distance` is textbook two-row Levenshtein; the name is generic. Clean's
  index numbering does **not** left-pad to width (unlike wf.rs `numbered_index_
  width`, wf.rs:1353/431), a divergence. → **Filtered.**
- **Public names** (checks.rs:588-654): API-given `public_names_report` /
  `public_names_report_from_pairs` (required_api.md:21-22). Header text observed
  (p32/p38; BEHAVIOR.md:74-81). Clean's pair order is **`(name, rule)`** — the
  *opposite* of wf.rs's **`(rule, name)`** (wf.rs:1455) — and clean groups by
  first-seen order, whereas both Haskell (`clashesOn` → `sortOn`) and wf.rs sort
  by lowercased name. Strong divergence. → **Filtered; independence evidence.**
- **Formula terms** (checks.rs:657-755): the `` `Free x' `` rendering was
  observed live (QUERIES.log p05 `-> "Formula terms" (\`Free y')`), and the help
  text appears in `oracle/captures/…Typing_and_Destructors…out`. Clean tracks a
  binder stack (`free_vars_formula` push/pop on quantifiers) because
  `ast_types.rs` represents quantified vars by *name* (`Forall(Vec<VarSpec>,…)`),
  not de Bruijn — so the stack is forced by the data model. Haskell `checkTerms`
  (Wellformedness.hs:960-985) needs no stack because its LNFormula already carries
  `Bound`/`Free`. `atom_vars` (checks.rs:696-712) maps each `Atom` variant to its
  constituent terms — forced by the `Atom` enum in `ast_types.rs:311-320` — and
  even diverges from Haskell `atomTerms` (Wellformedness.hs:908-915) by including
  predicate-atom facts (Haskell returns `[]` for `Syntactic`). → **Filtered.**
- **Formula guardedness** (checks.rs:761-836): the message body
  ("cannot be converted to a guarded formula" / "unguarded variable(s) … in the
  subformula … in the formula") is documented as observed (QUERIES.log
  p21/p39; BEHAVIOR.md:140-145). The detection (`find_unguarded`/`guard_vars`:
  a quantifier is "guarded" if its bound vars all appear under some Action/Pred
  atom) is a self-declared partial heuristic and is nothing like Haskell's real
  `formulaToGuarded` conversion. → **Filtered: observed output; own algorithm.**
- **Nat Sorts** (checks.rs:842-888): message observed (t_nat; BEHAVIOR.md:147).
  Clean collects offending *vars* (sort ≠ Nat) under a `%+`; Haskell
  `nonWellSorted` (Wellformedness.hs:293-304) collects offending *terms* recursing
  through nested nat-plus. Different traversal. → **Filtered.**
- **Subterm convergence** (checks.rs:894-930): intro + manual-URL string appears
  in captures (OIDC/POIDC/eccDAA). `is_subterm` is a self-declared structural
  approximation, unrelated to Haskell's `filterNonSubtermCtxtRule`. → **Filtered.**

## 4. pretty.rs

Every rule traces to an observed probe (t_terms/t_xor/t_nat/f_nullary;
BEHAVIOR.md §"Term pretty-printer"). Sort sigils `~ $ % #`, pair flattening
`<…>`, `f(a, b)`, `'lit'`, `^` without parens, AC operators parenthesized,
`%1`, and `DH_neutral` (present in `oracle/captures/…RYY_PFS…out`) are all
observed output. Independent-derivation signals vs. wf.rs:

- `Term::NumberOne => "1"` (pretty.rs:87) vs. wf.rs `NumberOne => "one"`
  (wf.rs:466). **Opposite rendering.**
- Only `Xor` operands are sorted, by **rendered-string** sort (pretty.rs:71-74);
  wf.rs sorts **all** AC operators (Mult/Union/Xor/NatPlus) by a term `Ord`
  (`cmp_wf_term`, wf.rs:517-521). Different scope and different comparator.

→ **Filtered: observed output; clear divergence from wf.rs.**

## 5. formula.rs

Single-line unicode printer `pp_formula` (formula.rs:10-42). Operator glyphs
(⊥ ⊤ ¬ ∧ ∨ ⇒ ⇔ ∀ ∃ = < ⋖ ⊏ @ last(…)) are formula-rendering output; the
module header states it was "calibrated from the oracle (e.g. ks1 lemma L2)".
Clean **fully parenthesizes** every binary connective (`({}) ∧ ({})`), unlike
Haskell's precedence-minimized `prettyLNFormula`. → **Filtered: observed/
calibrated; own parenthesization.** (`⋖` LessMset / `⊏` Subterm may be rarer in
the probed lemmas, but both are standard notation and the module is declared a
documented partial; not worth a redo.)

## 6. Secondary check — divergence from replaced `wf.rs`

Instruction: flag only where clean matches `wf.rs` structure in ways Haskell and
behavior do not explain. **Result: no such match.** Every clean/wf.rs match is
covered by `required_api.md` (WfError, new, WfReport, insert_wf_before,
after_public_names_topics, public_names_report[_from_pairs], topics,
underline_topic, render_report, check_theory, check_if_lemmas_in_theory,
fact_lhs_occur_no_rhs). The clean code diverges from wf.rs everywhere the API is
silent:

| Aspect | clean | wf.rs |
|---|---|---|
| `_from_pairs` tuple order | `(name, rule)` | `(rule, name)` |
| public-names grouping | first-seen order | sorted (`clashesOn`) |
| `NumberOne` render | `"1"` | `"one"` |
| AC operand sort | Xor only, string sort | all AC, term `Ord` |
| `underline_topic` trailing `\n` | none | present |
| numbered index padding | none | width-padded |
| internal sort order (`sort_rank`) | Msg<Pub<Fresh<Node<Nat | Pub<Fresh<Msg<Node<Nat |
| check fn names | topic-derived | port-derived |

No Haskell-internal identifiers or `.hs:` source citations appear anywhere in the
clean sources (grep confirmed empty).

## 7. Process caveat (NOT a clean-side violation; no redo)

`required_api.md` hands the clean room three functions whose *existence* is a
port-specific implementation seam with **no behavioral justification derivable
from the oracle**: `insert_wf_before`, `after_public_names_topics`, and the
`public_names_report` / `public_names_report_from_pairs` split. The oracle never
exposes these seams; the clean room implemented them only because the interface
required them. Per the protocol these are "API names given in the provided
interface docs" and are explicitly *not* clean-room violations, so no redo is
issued to `wf-clean`. This is flagged upward to the process owners: the
interoperability header for future clusters should prefer conveying *behavior/
ordering* over port-internal function decompositions, to keep the clean room from
inheriting the port's architecture. The clean implementations of these functions
are, in any case, independently expressed (see §1, §2, §3).
